author: swar8080
description: Know when installed plugins have updates and evaluate the risk of upgrading.

manifest.author: Obsidian
manifest.description: Know when installed plugins have updates and evaluate the risk of upgrading
manifest.name: Plugin Update Tracker

name: Plugin Update Tracker

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
- New setting "Show Notification on New Update" to show a small pop-up in the corner of the screen when there are updates. This is useful on mobile since the plugin icon is less visible (implemented by @ckt1031).
- Fixes some typos in error messages (fixed by @claremacrae).
releases.name: v1.7.0 optional notification on new plugin updates

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
- Fixes regressions introduced by 1.6.1 with detecting plugin enablement and incorrectly enabling disabled plugins after updating them
- Further improves interaction with `lazy-plugin`. When this plugin detects that `lazy-plugin` and the *Ignore Updates to Disabled Plugins* setting are enabled, this plugin's icon will stay in the loading state for an extra 10 seconds before actually checking for updates. This helps avoid timing issues where this plugin sees a plugin is disabled but then lazy-plugin enables it.
releases.name: V1.6.2

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
Fixes an issue where a plugin becomes disabled after updating it, specifically when a plugin like [lazy-plugin](https://github.com/alangrainger/obsidian-lazy-plugins) is also being used to speed-up obsidian start time

Thanks @alangrainger for pointing out the issue
releases.name: V1.6.1

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
- Release note text can now be selected and copied (thanks @jaycenhorton)
- Raw URLs in release notes like https://github.com/swar8080/obsidian-plugin-update-tracker are now rendered as links (thanks @jaycenhorton and @claremacrae)
- Disabled plugins with updates now have an indicator beside their name to remind you they're disabled
- After failing to check for updates because of an internet issue, you can now click the plugin icon to try again. Previously you had to restart obsidian or wait until the next scheduled update check
- Updates some dependencies that had CVEs. Most issues were for this plugin's development tooling and backend API
releases.name: V1.6.0

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
- Clicking the plugin icon when no updates are available now shows a small message making it clearer that everything's up-to-date. The cursor no longer disappears when hovering over the plugin icon (#72)
- Updates the description for the setting _Minimum update count to show a plugin icon_ to mention that updates for this plugin are always shown (in-case there's a bug preventing you from seeing other updates) (#67)
- Clearer message when your IP address exceeds github's limit of 60 file downloads per hour when updating plugins. The number of minutes until the limit is reset is included in the message (#73)
- Cleans-up some error logs in the developer console (#68)
- Increases the max number of your installed plugins checked for updates from 350 to 400 (if this is still too low then let me know)
releases.name: V1.5.2

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description: Fixes a bug introduced in 1.5.0 that causes the plugin to fail to load when freshly installed
releases.name: v1.5.1 Fixes Error Loading Plugin on New Installs

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description: **Change how often plugins are checked for updates** Currently this plugin checks for updates every 30 minutes. If you find the ⌛ loading icon distracting then you can use this new setting: **Minimum update count to show plugin icon** The default value is 0 but will be 1 if you currently have the "Hide plugin icon if no updates are available" setting enabled
releases.name: v1.5.0

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
- Fixes an issue detecting new plugin versions that use incomplete version numbers (ex: 2.7 instead of 2.7.0)
- On IPhone, the _Update Plugins_ button will now appear in the middle of the screen so that it can actually be tapped (fixing #49)
releases.name: v1.4.6 bug fixes

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description: Fixes a bug where the latest version of a plugin is installed even though an earlier version is shown in the UI (reported by @meskill)
releases.name: v1.4.5 fixes a bug installing an earlier version of a plugin

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
- Adds some padding to the _Update Plugins_ button to prevent it from being blocked by the status bar
- Adds Stable CSS selectors for the plug icon and status icon to the right of it
releases.name: v1.4.4 fixes update plugin button being cut-off by status bar

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description: Downloading plugin updates now works on mobile
releases.name: v1.4.3 fixes downloading plugin updates on mobile

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description: On desktop, this plugin update list will now open in a new tab instead of replacing the currently active tab
releases.name: v1.4.2 open plugin update list in a new tab

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
Plugin updates that are beta versions will no longer be shown by default. This can be changed in this plugin's settings:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17691679/204404186-f0cd0179-afd9-488b-b4f4-db15c72bfdae.png)

When choosing to see beta versions, they will have a "beta" indicator beside their name:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17691679/204404615-d0e31db8-944a-4ddc-9104-8b95e1160c39.png)
releases.name: v1.4.0 - Ignore and Identify Plugin Beta Versions

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description: HTML in release notes will now be rendered, mainly because github may use `` within release notes instead of markdown syntax All HTML is safely sanitized using the [same approach as github](https://github.com/rehypejs/rehype-sanitize)
releases.name: v1.3.1 fix img/html rendering in release notes

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
**Optionally hide the plugin icon when no updates are available** (suggested by [chrisgrieser](https://github.com/chrisgrieser)):

![Settings](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17691679/202867410-8db3b025-3b16-48ff-958d-17605a84bc1b.png)

**Adds CSS Selectors that can be used with Obsidian's CSS Snippets for further customization** (suggested by [mdrain](https://github.com/mdrain)):

![CSS Selectors](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17691679/202867470-97ff46ba-fd04-4b1c-bf5b-a719e8a332f6.png)
releases.name: v1.3.0 - Plugin Appearance Customizations

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description: Fix: Closes the plugin update list on mobile when opening another note
releases.name: v1.2.1

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description: ## Icon Shown on Mobile/Tablet The action ribbon will now display this icon on mobile/tablet when there's at least 1 update available: ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17691679/201531929-05444ddd-0a5a-4f65-b5f8-f0576679b65c.png) Note that the update count is currently not shown on mobile/tablet. This icon can also be hidden from this plugin's settings to avoid clutter on your action ribbon: ## Ignore Updates to Disabled Plugins A setting has been added so that installed but disabled plugins are not tracked by this plugin
releases.name: v1.2.0 - Icon on mobile/tablet & Disabled Plugin Filter

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description: Fixes an issue enabling this plugin on ipads and iphones - #5
releases.name: v1.1.1 - Fix plugin enablement on ios ipad/iphone

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
Hide new plugin versions that you don't want to install from the plugin icon count and update list

If you change your mind, you can unhide the version by visiting this plugin's settings

[Click here for a video showing how it works](https://github.com/swar8080/obsidian-plugin-update-tracker#ignore-specific-plugin-updates)
releases.name: v1.1.0 - Ignore Specific Plugin Versions

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
## Fixes
- Correct handling of beta versions installed using a tool like BRAT (#8)

## Other small usability improvements
- Adds a name to the tab displaying plugin updates so you can tell it apart from other note tabs that are open
- More noticeable tooltips and mouse cursors when hovering over parts of the UI
- Correct handling of tabs when clicking the plugin icon multiple times

## Coming soon
Filters ignoring updates for specific plugins, versions, etc.

Feel free to suggest other features/bug fixes [here](https://github.com/swar8080/obsidian-plugin-update-tracker/issues)
releases.name: v1.0.1

releases.author: swar8080
releases.description:
Install new versions of plugins that have updates:

![Screen_Recording_2022-10-29_at_3_43_48_PM_AdobeExpress (1)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17691679/198850942-bff3fbf5-1aa6-4195-a0df-15b9d4721a0b.gif)
releases.name: 1.0.0 - Install Plugin Updates
